label: MFCC Coefficient 2
reason: The 2nd Mel-frequency cepstral coefficient captures the overall spectral shape of the voice. Parkinson's patients show elevated values, reflecting changes in vocal tract resonance caused by muscle rigidity and reduced breath support.

label: Max Wavelet Energy (Level 12)
reason: Reduced peak energy at this level indicates weakened and less stable vocal fold vibrations, reflecting loss of strong, structured speech components in Parkinson’s patients.

label: Acceleration Variability (8th MFCC)
reason: The second-order rate of change (delta-delta) of the 8th MFCC measures how rapidly the spectral dynamics accelerate. Higher variability in Parkinson's patients indicates unstable, tremor-driven fluctuations in speech production.

label: Acceleration Variability (9th MFCC)
reason: Similar to the 8th coefficient, elevated delta-delta variability in the 9th MFCC reflects neuromotor instability. Parkinson's patients exhibit greater speech acceleration irregularities due to impaired motor control.

label: Min Wavelet Energy (Level 13)
reason: Increased minimum energy reflects elevated background fluctuations and irregular energy distribution, indicating vocal instability and tremor-related noise in Parkinson’s speech.
